title: neoporteria iquiquensis
intro: Neoporteria iquiquensis, also known as the Chilean coppery fern, is a type of plant species belonging to the fern family, with its unique copper-colored fronds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine in Chile for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Neoporteria iquiquensis include its potential to treat various conditions, such as arthritis, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for topical application, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
